title: Differential brain mechanisms of selection and maintenance of information during working memory (MEG data)
description: This magnetoencephalography (MEG) dataset investigates the differential neural mechanisms underlying selection and maintenance of information during working memory tasks. The study comprises MEG recordings from 22 participants (one participant from the original cohort of 23 did not sign a data sharing agreement) performing a working memory task and a one-back control task, complemented by structural MRI and detailed behavioral data. The dataset includes two experimental sessions per participant with synchronized MEG acquisition, eye-tracking validation, and head position indicator coordinates for source localization.
methods_description: MEG data were acquired during two sessions using the NIH MEG facility. Participants performed a working memory task involving visual Gabor stimuli (varying in spatial frequency and orientation) with directional cues, and a one-back control task (LocaCue). Behavioral responses and reaction times were recorded using MATLAB/Psychtoolbox 3. Head position indicator coordinates (nasion, left and right) were acquired via neuronavigation (Brainsight) and registered to structural MRI. Eye fixation was monitored throughout sessions.
